payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Source elements present in the ledger.

original_descriptions: Pocket money task with completion status and monetary value
Child performance report for compensation purposes
Enable children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly cycle
Provide children visibility into available and completed task inventory
Weekly cycle boundary for earned amount total calculation
Weekly task availability reset cycle timing

payload.evaluated_scope: All input material in this project

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming and completion tracking process
payload.justification: SG049 describes claiming and tracking as strategic processes performed by the enterprise to manage pocket money tasks.

payload.description: Task definition and monetary value assignment process
payload.justification: SG050 articulates the process by which parents define and assign monetary values to tasks at strategic level.

payload.description: Compensation distribution process based on performance reporting
payload.justification: SG053 establishes compensation distribution as a strategic process requiring performance data inputs.

payload.description: Child task completion and earnings tracking system with compensation details
payload.justification: Tasks are the central entity referenced across multiple signals, representing things of strategic importance tracked and compensated. | SG052 references earnings summary as a distinct strategic entity providing aggregated compensation information. | SG053 identifies performance reporting as a strategic entity used for compensation distribution decisions.

payload.description: Longitudinal task history for accountability tracking
payload.justification: SG054 references historical task data as a strategic entity supporting accountability and trend analysis.

payload.description: Weekly cycle for task claiming, tracking, and earnings calculation
payload.justification: SG049 and SG052 explicitly reference weekly periodicity as a strategic timing cycle for task and earnings management.

payload.description: Child user claiming and tracking pocket money tasks
payload.justification: Multiple signals (SG049, SG051, SG052) reference children as key actors performing task-related activities within the enterprise context.

payload.description: Parent user defining tasks and distributing compensation
payload.justification: SG050 and SG053 identify parents as strategic actors responsible for task definition and compensation management.

payload.description: Enable children to view available tasks and track completed pocket money tasks on a weekly cycle
payload.justification: SG049 explicitly expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling task claiming and tracking for children, directly mapping to a strategic goal. | SG051 captures the purpose of enabling task visibility for children, a clear strategic goal of transparency.

payload.description: Enable parents to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values
payload.justification: SG050 articulates parental motivation to define and associate monetary compensation with tasks, representing a strategic objective.

payload.description: Enable children to view their weekly earnings summary
payload.justification: SG052 identifies the motivational goal of earnings visibility, supporting accountability and motivation at strategic level.

payload.description: Enable parents to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution
payload.justification: SG053 establishes the parental need for performance reporting to support fair compensation, a clear strategic goal.

payload.description: Provide longitudinal task history access to support accountability and trend analysis
payload.justification: SG054 indicates stakeholder requirement for historical tracking, representing a strategic objective for oversight and analysis.

payload.description: Parent specifies tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: Parent capability to define task entities with associated monetary attributes is a business function.

payload.description: Child claims completed task for reward tracking
payload.justification: Core business function enabling children to claim completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.

payload.description: Calculate weekly earnings total for child
payload.justification: Weekly aggregation of earned monetary totals is a calculation function.

payload.description: Generate parent report showing child-task-earnings relationships
payload.justification: Parent reporting capability is explicitly defined as a business function supporting payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Task availability resets at weekly boundaries
payload.justification: Cyclical reset rule governs task availability at defined temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Historical task data must be retained and accessible
payload.justification: Mandatory retention and accessibility requirement is a business rule governing data management.

payload.description: Task entity (with states including available, completed, and associated monetary value)
payload.justification: Signal explicitly defines task entities with monetary value attributes specified by parents. | Completed tasks are distinct entities that children claim and that are linked to child entities. | Available tasks are distinct entities visible to child actors within temporal scope.

payload.description: Child actor entity
payload.justification: Child entities are explicitly referenced as participants linked to tasks and viewing task data.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Weekly aggregated monetary totals are data entities visible to child actors.

payload.description: Historical task data
payload.justification: Historical task data entities must be retained across temporal periods.

payload.description: Monetary value of task
payload.justification: Monetary value is explicitly defined as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Child claims completed task
payload.justification: Core business relationship where child entities claim completed task entities for reward tracking. | Signal explicitly specifies linkage relationship between completed task and child entities.

payload.description: Child-task-earnings association for parent reporting
payload.justification: Reporting capability requires a three-way relationship between child, task, and earnings entities.

payload.description: Task completion claimed by child
payload.justification: Child claiming a completed task is a significant business event triggering reward tracking.

payload.description: Weekly period for task availability and earnings aggregation
payload.justification: Weekly temporal cycle governs both task reset and earnings aggregation.

payload.description: Weekly boundary triggers task availability reset
payload.justification: Weekly temporal boundary acts as a trigger for cyclical task availability reset.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Child actors are explicitly identified as users who claim tasks, view tasks, and see earnings.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Parent actors are explicitly identified as users who specify tasks and view reporting for payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Track monetary rewards earned by children for completed tasks
payload.justification: Core business concept establishes the goal of tracking children's monetary rewards from task completion.

payload.description: Support parent payment fulfillment through reporting
payload.justification: Reporting capability explicitly serves the goal of supporting payment fulfillment by parents.

payload.description: Child visibility limited to available and completed tasks within temporal scope
payload.justification: Visibility requirements define constraints on what child actors can see within defined temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Historical task data must remain accessible across temporal periods
payload.justification: Mandatory retention and accessibility across time periods is a constraint on data management.

payload.description: Weekly aggregation function computing earned amounts for children
payload.justification: Signal describes temporal aggregation logic that computes weekly earnings, representing a distinct logical function in the processing model.

payload.description: Reporting interface function providing parents aggregated child earnings and completion data
payload.justification: Signal defines a logical reporting interface that aggregates and presents data, representing a function that transforms data for parent actor consumption.

payload.description: Logical association between task completion event and child entity
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a logical association rule linking task completion events to child entities, directly mapping to a Relationship classification in the data model.

payload.description: Data visibility scope attribute controlling child user access to task collections
payload.justification: Signal defines data visibility requirements that constrain logical access patterns, representing an attribute governing data accessibility in the logical model.

payload.description: Weekly cycle governing task availability restoration
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior, directly mapping to a temporal Cycle classification.

payload.description: Historical task record persistence constraint requiring logical data retention
payload.justification: Signal mandates data retention requirements for historical records, representing a design constraint on system behavior and data lifecycle.

payload.description: Operating system version compatibility constraint for platform deployment
payload.justification: Signal explicitly describes a platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete OS versions for deployment. This is a technical constraint governing deployment decisions at the Builder level.

payload.description: Weekly cycle boundary for task availability reset and earned amount total calculation
payload.justification: SG542 establishes weekly operational schedule boundary for earned amount access; at Row 5 this defines the detailed timing specification for weekly total calculations. | SG544 specifies weekly operational cycle timing for task reset operations; at Row 5 this represents the detailed sequencing specification for periodic task refresh.

payload.description: iOS mobile platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 defines deployment platform locations including iOS; per Rule 5 each named platform produces a separate Node CCI representing a distinct physical deployment target. | SG545 defines deployment platform locations including Android; per Rule 5 each named platform produces a separate Node CCI representing a distinct physical deployment target.

payload.description: Windows desktop platform deployment node
payload.justification: SG545 defines deployment platform locations including Windows; per Rule 5 each named platform produces a separate Node CCI representing a distinct physical deployment target.

payload.description: Child user interface for task claiming operations
payload.justification: SG539 identifies children as operational actors executing task claiming activities; at Row 5 this translates to the detailed user interface specification for child interactions.

payload.description: Parent user interface for task configuration and value assignment
payload.justification: SG540 identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary assignment; at Row 5 this represents the detailed interface specification for parent administrative functions.

payload.description: Child user interface for viewing available and completed task lists
payload.justification: SG541 defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing tasks; at Row 5 this specifies the detailed interface for task visibility and status display.

payload.description: Parent user interface for accessing earned amount reports
payload.justification: SG543 defines parent operational role in accessing reports for distribution activities; at Row 5 this represents the detailed reporting interface specification.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the fundamental purpose of enabling children to claim and track completed pocket money tasks weekly.
Articulates parental motivation to define compensated task assignments with associated monetary values.
Captures the purpose of providing children visibility into available and completed task inventory.
Identifies the motivational goal of enabling children to view their weekly earnings summary.
Establishes parental need to access child performance reporting for accurate compensation distribution purposes.
Indicates stakeholder requirement for longitudinal task history access supporting accountability or trend analysis.
Section header indicating the commencement of conceptual system description.
Establishes the core business concept of children claiming completed tasks for monetary reward tracking.
Defines the parent capability to specify task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Specifies the relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage.
Defines child actor visibility requirements for both available and completed task entities within temporal scope.
Establishes weekly aggregation capability for earned monetary totals visible to child actors.
Defines parent reporting capability displaying child-task-earnings relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Specifies the cyclical business rule for task availability reset at weekly temporal boundaries.
Mandates retention and accessibility of historical task data entities across temporal periods.
Logical association rule defining how task completion events are linked to child entities.
Data visibility requirements specifying logical access patterns for child users to task collections.
Temporal aggregation logic defining weekly computation and presentation of earned amounts to children.
Logical reporting interface providing parents aggregated view of child earnings and task completion data.
Periodic state reset logic defining weekly cycle behavior for task availability restoration.
Data retention requirement mandating logical persistence and accessibility of historical task records.
Platform compatibility constraint specifying concrete operating system versions for deployment.
Identifies children as operational actors who execute task claiming activities throughout the week on a temporal basis.
Identifies parents as operational actors responsible for task configuration and monetary value assignment activities.
Defines operational visibility requirements for child actors viewing available and completed tasks within weekly cycles.
Establishes weekly operational schedule boundary when child actors can access earned amount totals.
Defines parent operational role in accessing reports to execute pocket money distribution activities.
Specifies weekly operational cycle timing when task availability resets for claiming operations.
Defines deployment platform locations specifying where the system operates across iOS, Android, and Windows environments.
